LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 23, 2014 FOR THE FOLLOWING REASONS: (1) THE COURT IS NOT IN RECEIPT OF COURTESY COPIES OF THE PETITION FILED ON DECEMBER 20, 2011 AND THE MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED ON NOVEMBER 8, 2013. PETITIONER MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THESE PAPERS TO DEPT. 302 NO LATER THAN TWO WEEKS PRIOR TO THE NEW HEARING DATE WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. (SEE LOCAL RULE 2.6B.) (2) THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD IS COMPRISED OF 95 EXHIBITS, BUT THE EXHIBITS ARE NOT SEPARATED BY EXHIBIT TABS, AS REQUIRED BY CRC 3.1110(F). THE INDEX TO ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD DOES NOT IDENTIFY THE BATES RANGE FOR EACH EXHIBIT. PETITIONER MUST DELIVER AN ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD WITH EXHIBIT TABS, AND A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN TWO WEEKS PRIOR TO THE NEW HEARING DATE. P ETITIONER MAY PICK UP THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD THAT DEPT. 302 IS CURRENTLY IN RECEIPT OF IF HE WISHES. PLEASE CONTACT THE COURT CLERK AT (415) 551-3723 TO MAKE ARRANGEMENTS FOR PICK UP. (3) THE COURT REQUESTS THAT PETITIONER ENSURE THAT ALL HEARING TRANSCRIPTS HAVE BEEN PROVIDED TO THE COURT. THE COURT NOTES THAT IT HAS RECEIVED A CD CONTAINING AUDIO RECORDING OF A JUNE 16, 2010 HEARING, BUT THERE DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE A CORRESPONDING TRANSCRIPT IN THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG'S (AMENDED) PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE - THE HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS OFF CALENDAR. ON MAY 8, 2014, AND JUNE 23, 2014, THE COURT ORDERED PETITIONER TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF HIS PAPERS AND PROPERLY TABBED EXHIBITS IN COMPLIANCE WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B AND CRC 1110 (F) NOT LESS THAN TWO WEEKS PRIOR TO THE NEW HEARING DATES. ON AUGUST 11, 2014, THE COURT RECEIVED COURTESY COPIES OF THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE AND THE MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, AND A LETTER FROM PETITIONER STATING THAT PETITIONER WOULD BE SUBMITTING A NEW COPY OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD WITH INDEX TABS AND A MORE DETAILED INDEX "HOPEFULLY BY THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS" ON AUGUST 11, 2014. THESE DOCUMENTS WERE UNTIMELY GIVEN THE TWO PRIOR ORDERS CONTINUING THE HEARING. PETITIONER MUST RE-NOTICE A HEARING ON THE PETITION IN ORDER TO OBTAIN A NEW HEARING DATE, AND MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS AND PROPERLY TABBED EXHIBITS IN COMPLIANCE WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B AND CRC 1110(F). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

PETITIONER'S MOTION WAS PREVIOUSLY TAKEN OFF CALENDAR ON AUGUST 12, 2014 FOR FAILURE TO PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. BECAUSE THE MOTION WAS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR, THE COURT NO LONGER HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE PAPERS FILED IN THIS MATTER. ALL PARTIES SHALL DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE PAPERS THEY FILED TO DEPT. 302 BY OCTOBER 9, 2014 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT IS IN RECEIPT OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD PROVIDED BY PETITIONER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
Re-Notrice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-23-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JANELLE E. CAYWOOD, TEL. NO. 928-4102 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. LISA BERKOWITZ, TEL. NO. 554-3825 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate is denied. The admission of the Cantamount testimony and invoice (Exhibit 29) does not mandate reversal of respondent's decision. In Thornbrough v. W. Placer Unified Sch. Dist. (2013) 223 Cal.App.4th 169, 200, the court explains: " 'Error of law is not reversible unless, on an examination of the record, it appears to have resulted in a miscarriage of justice.' " [citation] And it is well-settled that the improper admission or rejection of evidence at an administrative hearing does not provide "grounds for reversal unless the error has resulted in a miscarriage of justice. [Citation.] In other words, it must be reasonably probable a more favorable result would have been reached absent the error." The Wilson violations alone (specifications 4 and 5) support the termination of petitioner. The record demonstrates that petitioner illegally accessed CLETS in 2005 and was guilty of the same violation with regard to the Wilson brothers several years later. Respondent did not abuse its discretion in rejecting petitioner's claim that he was performing a proper police investigation. It is noteworthy that petitioner never contacted the Gang Task Force, which was the body responsible for such an investigation. In addition, the record showed that Officer Lozada contacted Hayward Police and attempted to obtain information regarding the Wilsons, but the Hayward officer did not provide any information because he was unsure regarding Officer Lozada's status. It is reasonable to assume that Officer Lozada probably engaged in the same type of conduct with petitioner.
The court does not find petitioner's other arguments persuasive. While the commissioners are supposed to affirm on the record that they reviewed all of the evidence at the guilt phase (Rule XII (B)(4)), there is no reason to believe that the commissioners actually failed to review all of the evidence. There is no support for petitioner's argument that the same commissioners need to preside over the guilt and penalty phases. The commission rules do not support that assertion, respondent's interpretation of its own rules is entitled to judicial deference, and such an interpretation rule would be unworkable given the turnover at the Police Commission and other circumstances that may lead to a commissioner being unavailable.
ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =(302/EHG)

Re-Notrice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-06-2014 Continued To Oct-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG's Re-Noticed Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate is continued to October 23, 2014 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Petitioner's motion was previously taken off calendar on August 12, 2014 for failure to provide a courtesy copy of the administrative record. Because the motion was taken off calendar, the Court no longer has courtesy copies of the papers filed in this matter. All parties shall deliver courtesy copies of the papers they filed to Dept. 302 by October 9, 2014 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. The Court is in receipt of the administrative record provided by Petitioner. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

(Amended) Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG's (Amended) Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate - The hearing on petition for writ of mandate is off calendar. On May 8, 2014, and June 23, 2014, the court ordered petitioner to provide courtesy copies of his papers and properly tabbed exhibits in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B and CRC 1110 (f) not less than two weeks prior to the new hearing dates. On August 11, 2014, the Court received courtesy copies of the Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandate and the Memorandum of Points and Authorities, and a letter from Petitioner stating that Petitioner would be submitting a new copy of the administrative record with index tabs and a more detailed index "hopefully by the close of business" on August 11, 2014. These documents were untimely given the two prior orders continuing the hearing. Petitioner must re-notice a hearing on the Petition in order to obtain a new hearing date, and must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers and properly tabbed exhibits in compliance with Local Rule 2.6B and CRC 1110(f). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM

(Amended) Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-23-2014 Continued To Aug-12-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG's (Amended) Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate is continued to August 12, 2014 for the following reasons: (1) The Court is not in receipt of Courtesy Copies of the Petition filed on December 20, 2011 and the Memorandum of Points and Authorities filed on November 8, 2013. Petitioner must deliver courtesy copies of these papers to Dept. 302 no later than two weeks prior to the new hearing date with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. (See Local Rule 2.6B.) (2) The Administrative Record is comprised of 95 exhibits, but the exhibits are not separated by exhibit tabs, as required by CRC 3.1110(f). The Index to Administrative Record does not identify the bates range for each exhibit. Petitioner must deliver an administrative record with exhibit tabs, and a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date, no later than two weeks prior to the new hearing date. Petitioner may pick up the administrative record that Dept. 302 is currently in receipt of if he wishes. Please contact the Court Clerk at (415) 551-3723 to make arrangements for pick up. (3) The Court requests that Petitioner ensure that all hearing transcripts have been provided to the Court. The Court notes that it has received a CD containing audio recording of a June 16, 2010 hearing, but there does not appear to be a corresponding transcript in the Administrative Record. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm
